commands.review_summary:
Adds a GatewayClient `beginStop()` `_socket.unref()` call after `ws.close()` plus two colocated Vitest tests for socket unref and missing `_socket` behavior.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +74. Total +86 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence local reproduction was established in this read-only review. The linked issue provides timeout and file-descriptor evidence, and source inspection confirms the reported `message send` path reaches GatewayClient shutdown, but no live Discord send was run here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Keep this in human review until the contributor or a maintainer supplies real behavior proof for the reported CLI hang; ClawSweeper automation cannot prove the contributor's local Discord/gateway setup for them.

target.title: fix(gateway-client): unref WS socket in beginStop() to fix CLI hang (#88230)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw message send` hangs after successful delivery because the gateway client's WebSocket socket stays ref'd in the Node event loop. `beginStop()` calls `ws.close()` and starts the close handshake, but the underlying `_socket` file descriptor remains ref'd, so Node's event loop never drains to zero active handles and a natural process exit never happens.

This adds a single `.unref()` call on `ws._socket` immediately after `ws.close()` inside `beginStop()`, wrapped in try/catch because `_socket` is an internal property of the `ws` package with no public contract.

Closes #88230.

## Why a new PR (supersedes #88282)

PR #88282 targeted the old `src/gateway/client.ts` location. After the package refactor that moved `GatewayClient` into `packages/gateway-client/`, the PR went dirty/conflicting against main and was flagged `needs-real-behavior-proof`. This is the same one-line behavioral fix, rebased onto the new location, with a smaller targeted test that lives next to the implementation. I'll close #88282 once this is in your review queue.

## Verification

- New file: `packages/gateway-client/src/client.socket-unref.test.ts`
  - `unrefs the underlying socket on stop() so the event loop can drain` — pins the behavioral fix
  - `tolerates a ws without an internal _socket (defensive contract)` — confirms the try/catch path
  - Same ws-injection pattern as the existing `client.watchdog.test.ts`
- `vitest run packages/gateway-client/` — **50/50 pass** locally (12 watchdog + 10 timeouts + 1 event-loop-ready + 2 new socket-unref + workspace duplicates)

## Why this is safe

- `stopAndWait()` semantics unchanged — still resolves via the close event or force-terminate fallback
- `forceTerminateTimer.unref()` was already in place; this matches its pattern
- Try/catch ensures forward-compatibility if `ws` ever removes `_socket`
- Public TypeScript types not touched

🪶
target.author: bartok9
